questions.question: Explain the purpose of glutCreateMenu() and list its parameter and return type.
keyPoints: glutCreateMenu() creates a new pop-up menu and returns a unique integer identifier (menu ID)
Takes a function pointer to a callback function as parameter: void (*func)(int value)
The callback function is invoked when any menu item is selected
The returned menu ID is used in other menu functions like glutAddSubMenu()

questions.question: Describe the difference between glutAddMenuEntry() and glutAddSubMenu() with example syntax.
keyPoints: glutAddMenuEntry() adds a selectable item that triggers callback with a value
Syntax: glutAddMenuEntry(const char *name, int value)
glutAddSubMenu() adds a cascading submenu that opens another menu
Syntax: glutAddSubMenu(const char *name, int submenu)
Menu entry executes action; submenu navigates to another menu

questions.question: Write the sequence of GLUT function calls required to create a pop-up menu attached to the right mouse button.
keyPoints: glutCreateMenu(callbackFunction) - create menu and set callback
glutAddMenuEntry() - add menu items with values
glutAttachMenu(GLUT_RIGHT_BUTTON) - attach to right mouse button
The right-click now triggers the menu at cursor position

questions.question: What is a menu callback function? How is it invoked and what information does it receive?
keyPoints: A callback function is user-defined and registered with glutCreateMenu()
It is automatically invoked when a menu item is selected
Receives a single integer parameter: the value associated with selected menu entry
Application uses this value with switch/if-else to determine action

questions.question: Explain the concept of context menus (pop-up menus) in computer graphics applications.
keyPoints: Context menus appear at cursor position when triggered (typically right-click)
They provide context-sensitive options based on current interaction
Also called pop-up or shortcut menus
Allow quick access to frequently used commands without navigating menus

questions.question: Design and explain a complete GLUT menu system for a 2D drawing application that includes options for drawing different shapes (point, line, triangle, circle), color selection (red, green, blue), and clearing the screen.
keyPoints: Need multiple menus: Shapes (with submenu), Colors (with submenu), Actions (Clear, Exit)
Create submenus first using glutCreateMenu()
Use glutAddSubMenu() to add hierarchical structure
Use glutAddMenuEntry() for final action items
Use switch statements in callback to handle all value combinations
Proper menu organization improves user experience
Include proper callback handling for each selection

questions.question: Explain the event-driven programming model as applied to GLUT menus. Include in your explanation how user interactions flow through the system and how the callback mechanism works.
keyPoints: Event-driven programming: program waits for user events (clicks, keypresses)
GLUT main loop monitors for events and dispatches to appropriate handlers
Right-click triggers menu event → GLUT captures → displays menu at cursor
User selection generates menu event → GLUT calls registered callback with value
Callback executes application-specific code based on value
Separation of event detection (GLUT) and handling (user code) via callbacks
Enables modular, responsive graphics applications

questions.question: Compare hierarchical (cascading) menus with flat menus in terms of advantages and disadvantages. How would you decide which to use in a graphics application?
keyPoints: Flat menus: all items visible at once, simple to navigate, but limited by screen space
Hierarchical menus: organize many options into categories, scalable but require more clicks
Cascading menus use glutAddSubMenu() to create nested structure
Use hierarchical when: many menu items (>10), clear categorization exists, space is limited
Use flat when: few items, quick access important, simple application
GLUT supports both through menu creation functions
Decision based on: number of options, user frequency, organizational clarity
